{"id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.2","title":"Gateway: Yggdrasil daemon integration","description":"## Overview

Run Yggdrasil on gateway nodes to join the global backbone.

## Implementation options

### Option 1: External daemon (RPi/Linux)
- Run stock yggdrasil binary
- Configure via yggdrasil.conf
- Gateway communicates via TUN interface
- Simple, well-tested

### Option 2: Embedded library (ESP32/nRF)
- yggdrasil-go can be compiled as library (maybe)
- Or: port core routing to C
- Complex but self-contained

### Option 3: Yggdrasil-lite
- Implement minimal Yggdrasil client
- Just enough to peer and route
- No full DHT, rely on peers

**Recommendation:**
- RPi gateway: Option 1 (external daemon)
- Embedded gateway: Option 3 (lite client), or proxy via RPi

## Configuration

### Peering
```yaml
Peers:
  - tcp://ygg.mkg20001.io:80
  - tcp://yggpeer.example.com:12345
  # Or private peers only
```

### Private key
Import from LICHEN identity:
```yaml
PrivateKey: <LICHEN Ed25519 private key in Yggdrasil format>
```

### Subnet routing
Advertise mesh prefix:
```yaml
# Gateway announces that it can reach these addresses
# (mesh nodes under this gateway)
```

## Zephyr integration (embedded)

If running on Zephyr (not Linux):
- Need Yggdrasil-lite in C
- Or: use serial/IP bridge to external Yggdrasil
- Evaluate complexity vs just requiring RPi for global backbone

## Systemd service (Linux gateway)

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Yggdrasil for LICHEN gateway
After=network.target

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/bin/yggdrasil -useconffile /etc/lichen/yggdrasil.conf
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:42:18.236961Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T20:42:18.236961Z","labels":["gateway","yggdrasil","global","networking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.2","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:42:18.374964Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.2","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:42:18.488086Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"acceptance_criteria":"Specify and prototype a per-node identity-preserving Yggdrasil participation model; demonstrate bidirectional traffic through an unmodified Yggdrasil network with source /128 validation; nodes retain private keys; no gateway-owned 0300::/8 substitution; add independent interop tests before enabling non-local native routing in the baseline.","design":"A stock gateway-owned yggdrasil-go daemon cannot forward native 0200::/8 packets sourced from or destined to another node identity; it accepts only its own AddrForKey identity and routed 0300::/8 subnet. LICHEN chose stable per-node native /128s and rejects gateway-dependent 0300::/8 addressing. Design must therefore make each constrained node a logical Yggdrasil participant under its own key, without exporting node private keys to the gateway. Evaluate a constrained transport/control-plane split, not TUN proxying or source spoofing.","title":"Yggdrasil: Design per-node identity-preserving transport"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.2","title":"Yggdrasil: Design per-node identity-preserving transport","description":"## Overview

Run Yggdrasil on gateway nodes to join the global backbone.

## Implementation options

### Option 1: External daemon (RPi/Linux)
- Run stock yggdrasil binary
- Configure via yggdrasil.conf
- Gateway communicates via TUN interface
- Simple, well-tested

### Option 2: Embedded library (ESP32/nRF)
- yggdrasil-go can be compiled as library (maybe)
- Or: port core routing to C
- Complex but self-contained

### Option 3: Yggdrasil-lite
- Implement minimal Yggdrasil client
- Just enough to peer and route
- No full DHT, rely on peers

**Recommendation:**
- RPi gateway: Option 1 (external daemon)
- Embedded gateway: Option 3 (lite client), or proxy via RPi

## Configuration

### Peering
```yaml
Peers:
  - tcp://ygg.mkg20001.io:80
  - tcp://yggpeer.example.com:12345
  # Or private peers only
```

### Private key
Import from LICHEN identity:
```yaml
PrivateKey: <LICHEN Ed25519 private key in Yggdrasil format>
```

### Subnet routing
Advertise mesh prefix:
```yaml
# Gateway announces that it can reach these addresses
# (mesh nodes under this gateway)
```

## Zephyr integration (embedded)

If running on Zephyr (not Linux):
- Need Yggdrasil-lite in C
- Or: use serial/IP bridge to external Yggdrasil
- Evaluate complexity vs just requiring RPi for global backbone

## Systemd service (Linux gateway)

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Yggdrasil for LICHEN gateway
After=network.target

[Service]
ExecStart=/usr/bin/yggdrasil -useconffile /etc/lichen/yggdrasil.conf
Restart=always

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```","design":"A stock gateway-owned yggdrasil-go daemon cannot forward native 0200::/8 packets sourced from or destined to another node identity; it accepts only its own AddrForKey identity and routed 0300::/8 subnet. LICHEN chose stable per-node native /128s and rejects gateway-dependent 0300::/8 addressing. Design must therefore make each constrained node a logical Yggdrasil participant under its own key, without exporting node private keys to the gateway. Evaluate a constrained transport/control-plane split, not TUN proxying or source spoofing.","acceptance_criteria":"Specify and prototype a per-node identity-preserving Yggdrasil participation model; demonstrate bidirectional traffic through an unmodified Yggdrasil network with source /128 validation; nodes retain private keys; no gateway-owned 0300::/8 substitution; add independent interop tests before enabling non-local native routing in the baseline.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:42:18.236961Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-20T06:15:47.962095Z","labels":["gateway","yggdrasil","global","networking"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.2","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:42:18.374964Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.2","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-zt3c.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-07-16T20:42:18.488086Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
new_value: {"description":"Design and prototype per-node identity-preserving Yggdrasil participation for constrained LICHEN nodes. A stock gateway-owned daemon cannot proxy another node's native 0200::/8 source. Evaluate a control/data-plane split or constrained participant that keeps each node's private key on-node and interoperates with unmodified Yggdrasil."}

new_value: {"notes":"Community-facing work is planned in child project-LICHEN-zt3c.2.1. Yggdrasil has no formal RFC process: use #yggdrasil on Libera.Chat for reconnaissance, GitHub Ideas/Issues for durable design, and develop-branch PRs plus CHANGELOG/wire-version updates for accepted protocol work. All material IRC outcomes must be copied into the durable GitHub/design record. No upstream post, comment, issue, or PR without Mark's explicit authorization."}
